reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2: Trust Boundary - The attack path requires a compromised or malicious local Execution Layer (EL). The Engine API is explicitly defined as SEMI_TRUSTED in BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json with documented assumption PROP-6a4369e9-asm-002 ('Engine API is JWT-authenticated; blobs from local EL skip cryptographic verification'). The code behavior is intentional by design: new_for_block_publishing (L221-250) deliberately skips signature, KZG proof, inclusion proof, and parent block verification for EL-sourced columns, while full gossip validation (L474-529) performs all these checks. This design choice is documented in code comments (L218-220, L486-490). Attacks requiring a compromised local EL component are marked 'in_scope_for_bug_bounty: false' and fall outside the security model for this audit contest. The 01e property text from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json (L733-750) states 'After distributed blob publishing, imported columns must behave as if received via gossip' but the assertion qualifies it as 'forall col from EL' which inherently falls under the semi-trusted EL boundary. Severity remains HIGH per original spec but finding is disputed as it requires compromised trusted component outside security model.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: After distributed blob publishing, imported columns must behave as if received via gossip. (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json, property PROP-6a4369e9-post-003)
